seoDescription: Laboratories, herbaria, and specialist units that underpin plant medicine research at the Centre for Plant Medicine Research.
lede: CPMR maintains specialist laboratories and reference collections that support phytochemistry, quality assessment, and collaborative research programmes.
introParagraphs: Facility profiles summarise capacity, equipment classes, and public access notes for partners assessing infrastructure. Department mini-sites provide additional programme context where units are hosted.
Profiles below are verified seed content for build-time routing; imagery and detailed specifications will expand as editorial sign-off completes.
safetyNote: Laboratory and collection areas are not open for casual public tours. Visiting researchers and partners must coordinate access through the collaboration pathway and approved institutional contacts.

facilities.name: Phytochemistry analytical laboratory
facilities.summary: Chromatography, extraction, and compound characterisation workflows supporting medicinal plant research and quality assessment.
facilities.imageAlt: Placeholder illustration for the phytochemistry analytical laboratory
facilities.publicNotes: Analytical work follows departmental SOPs. Sample submission for external partners is coordinated through research collaboration enquiries.

facilities.name: Scientific and technical services quality unit
facilities.summary: Reference methods, calibration support, and quality frameworks for institutional laboratory operations.
facilities.imageAlt: Placeholder illustration for the scientific and technical services quality unit

specs.label: Primary focus
specs.value: Laboratory quality systems and technical support

specs.label: Access
specs.value: Internal programmes and approved external studies

facilities.publicNotes: Capacity upgrades and framework documentation are published through the research projects and publications pathways when approved.

facilities.name: Medicinal plants reference collection
facilities.summary: Curated herbarium and reference specimens supporting identification, conservation, and research traceability.
facilities.publicNotes: Specimen handling follows conservation and biosafety policies. Public gallery content may surface selected materials when editorially approved.
